title: Mapo Tofu Vegetarian

seo.title: How to Make Mapo Tofu Vegetarian — Easy Chinese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Mapo Tofu Vegetarian. This easy Chinese recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Mapo Tofu Vegetarian
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Chinese Mapo Tofu Vegetarian recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Mapo Tofu Vegetarian — a beloved Chinese classic

description: This vegetarian Mapo Tofu is a fiery and flavorful Sichuan classic, reimagined without meat. Silken tofu cubes are bathed in a rich, spicy, and aromatic sauce featuring fermented bean paste and numbing Sichuan peppercorns. It is a comforting and satisfying dish that perfectly balances heat and umami, ideal for a hearty meal.

ingredients.notes: mixed with 3 tablespoons cold water for slurry

instructions.title: Prepare Tofu
instructions.text: Bring a pot of water to a boil. Carefully add the tofu cubes and blanch for 2-3 minutes. This helps firm up the tofu and removes any raw bean flavor. Gently remove the tofu with a slotted spoon and set aside to drain.

instructions.title: Make Sichuan Peppercorn Oil
instructions.text: Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium heat. Add the whole Sichuan peppercorns and fry for 2-3 minutes until fragrant but not burnt. Remove the peppercorns with a slotted spoon and discard them, leaving the infused oil in the wok.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Pastes
instructions.text: To the infused oil, add the Doubanjiang and chopped fermented black beans. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until the oil turns red and the paste is fragrant. Add the minced garlic and ginger, and stir-fry for another minute until aromatic.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable broth. Add the light soy sauce and sugar. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, stirring to combine all the flavors. Taste and adjust seasonings if necessary, aiming for a balance of spicy, savory, and slightly sweet.

instructions.title: Add Tofu and Thicken
instructions.text: Gently add the blanched tofu cubes to the simmering sauce. Allow the tofu to warm through for about 2-3 minutes, absorbing the flavors. Stir the cornstarch slurry once more, then slowly pour it into the wok while stirring continuously until the sauce thickens to your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the sauce is thick and glossy, remove the wok from the heat. Transfer the Mapo Tofu to a serving dish. Garnish generously with chopped fresh scallions. Serve immediately with steamed white rice for a truly authentic Chinese meal.

tips: Blanching the tofu before adding it to the sauce helps it firm up and prevents it from breaking apart during cooking, while also improving its texture and ability to absorb flavor.
For an extra layer of flavor and heat, you can add 1-2 teaspoons of chili oil or a pinch of ground chili flakes along with the Doubanjiang.
Always taste your sauce before adding the cornstarch slurry; adjust the salt, sugar, and spiciness to your preference. Remember that the flavors will mellow slightly once the tofu is added.
article: Mapo Tofu Vegetarian is a beloved Chinese Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.

recipeIngredient: 2 blocks (about 1.5 lbs) Firm or Silken Tofu
2 tablespoons Doubanjiang (Sichuan Pixian Broad Bean Paste)
